---
title: "Reproduce everything"
---

The project is a two-language pipeline: an R analysis engine produces the frozen
flagship science, and a Python layer builds the teaching datasets, the ladder
stages, and this site. Nothing in `analysis/output/` is ever regenerated by the
Python layer — a hash gate enforces it.

## Environment

| Tool | Version | Install |
|---|---|---|
| R | 4.5.2 | `Rscript analysis/install_dependencies.R` |
| Python | 3.13 | `pip install -e ".[discovery]"` |
| Build extras | torch, gcastle | `pip install -r app/requirements-build.txt` |
| Site | Quarto ≥ 1.4 | `quarto render site` |

## The R analysis (frozen science)

Run `Rscript analysis/run_all.R` — one command for the whole R pipeline (the four
dataset generators are consolidated into `analysis/generate.R`, and every stage
self-locates the repo so it runs both via `Rscript` and interactively). This
produces the NASA renal-stone simulation, the
frozen interventional truth (50,000 common-random-number sims), the XGBoost
learner, and the three attribution families — including the deliberate null
result (ordinary ≈ DAG-ordering SHAP) and the structural prototype (τ = 0.794).

## The Python pipeline (teaching + site)

```bash
python -m causal_shap.build all         # the whole flow, in order:
                                        #   teaching-data → discovery → complexity
                                        #   → causal-shap → validation → figures
                                        #   → glossary → validate
python -m causal_shap.build discovery   # …or any single stage
```

::: {.pullquote}
The `validate` stage hashes every file under `analysis/output/` against a
committed baseline and fails if anything changed or was added. Frozen science,
additive engineering.
:::

**Determinism.** Every seed lives in `causal_shap/seeds.py`. Non-torch artifacts
are bit-for-bit reproducible across runs; the CVAE (torch) is guarded by
tolerance checks and committed decoder weights rather than bit-identity.

## The site

```bash
quarto render site          # executes cheap chunks once, writes _freeze/
```

With `freeze: true`, code executes only on your machine; the committed `_freeze/`
cache means CI and GitHub Pages **never run Python**. Render once locally after
changing any executable chunk, then commit `_freeze/`.
